feedback.text: For far too long digital companies have been selling products as a digital license, withholding the right to take it away at any time they wish. For video games that require a constant online Internet connection this means that publishers of these games are entitled to take down a game and effectively prevent anyone from accessing their purchased game whenever they wish. This should not be allowed, as a purchase of a product, whether digital or not, should allow me to access it whenever I wish. I and many others wish that the Digital Fairness Act would include legislation about this practice of tearing away access to rightfully purchased video games and stop companies from killing games.
